directive: In core.json, replace the support-switch claim in lem:parametric-gain-support-cells with the following exact criterion and make its proof use identical language: “Fix the stated lexicographic tie-breaking rule and let \(\sigma_E(C)\) denote the selected, lexicographically first maximizing support on an open comparison-sign cell \(C\). Let \(\gamma_0\) be an interior boundary with adjacent cells \(C_-\) and \(C_+\). A named ordered pair \(\mathbf H\to\mathbf K\), with \(\mathbf H\ne\mathbf K\), is a genuine selected-support switch at \(\gamma_0\) if and only if \(F_{E,\mathbf H}(\gamma_0)=F_{E,\mathbf K}(\gamma_0)=\max_{\mathbf L}F_{E,\mathbf L}(\gamma_0)\), \(\sigma_E(C_-)=\mathbf H\), and \(\sigma_E(C_+)=\mathbf K\). Hence the pairwise cross-product polynomial vanishes at \(\gamma_0\), but vanishing and dominance alone are not sufficient; the two named supports must be the respective adjacent-cell selections. Reversing the orientation exchanges \(\mathbf H\) and \(\mathbf K\).” Replace the repeated iff in thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy with this same named-pair adjacent-selection criterion and synchronize its proof rather than merely referring to the old lemma wording. Remove ass:prespecified-score from that theorem’s depends_on list and from the assumption’s corresponding used_by entry; do not add a validity conclusion to the computational theorem and do not delete the assumption, which remains available to lem:finite-joint-validity and inferential-validity prose. Apply the same redundant-dependency cleanup wholesale to lem:negative-gain-cell-witness: remove ass:prespecified-score from depends_on and delete the proof’s claim that prespecification is needed for the numerical input’s admissibility. Regenerate writeup.tex from the corrected source so the lemma statement and proof, theorem statement and proof, dependency metadata, and negative-gain proof all agree. Make no other substantive changes: preserve the Fisher-sharp-null Rosenbaum product-law scope, rational encoded-input domain, field headline, strict-gain/equality/negative-gain trichotomy, and no-full-product-enumeration certificate, without strengthening assumptions or changing definitions.

Reframe the field-level claim away from generic MILP certification: add a proved theorem thm:opposite-tail-capability-separation-and-hardness under the existing frozen assumptions stating (i) the exact Rosenbaum/Rosenbaum–Krieger one-sided score-monotone candidate reduction does not extend to \(A_J\), witnessed by the existing \(n=1,m=4,\Gamma=2,t=1,s=(-2,0,1,2)\) instance, where the unique maximizing high set is the nonmonotone event set \(\{-2,1,2\}\), giving \(p_J=6/7\), while every upper- or lower-score-monotone high set gives at most \(5/6\); (ii) the Fogarty–Small moment-QCQP cannot determine \(p_J\), proved at \(n=1,m=4,\Gamma=1,t=1\) by the rational score vectors \((-1,-1,1,1)\) and \((-7/5,-1/5,1/5,7/5)\), which have the same mean \(0\) and variance \(1\) but joint-tail probabilities \(1\) and \(1/2\); and (iii) exact computation of \(p_J\) is \(\#P\)-hard even for reversal-symmetric matched pairs at \(\Gamma=1\) with integer scores, by reducing \(\#\mathrm{PARTITION}\): set pair \(i\)’s scores to \((-a_i,a_i)\) and \(t=1\), so \(p_J=1-N_0/2^n\), where \(N_0\) is the number of zero signed sums. State only \(\#P\)-hardness of exact value computation and polynomial verification in transcript length—do not claim an unconditional exponential certificate lower bound. Split these claims into supporting lemmas if needed, add their exact dependencies and citations to the DAG, and make thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy depend on them while retaining its current exact MILP proof as the constructive upper bound. Rewrite the TL;DR, all project_justification fields, related_work, comparator table, novelty justification, technical limitation, and corresponding writeup.tex prose so the headline is the exact capability separation plus matching hardness/verified exponential-time construction, explicitly naming Rosenbaum (1988), Rosenbaum–Krieger (1990)/Pimentel–Huang (2024, Theorem 3), and Fogarty–Small (2016); delete the unsupported blanket ‘no published result’ claim and do not present finite vertex reduction, non-materialization of a product table, or ordinary branch-and-bound as the novelty. Also carry forward and actually apply the prior mandatory directive: replace the overstated support-switch iff in lem:parametric-gain-support-cells and thm:operational-joint-certificate-and-gain-trichotomy by the exact fixed-lexicographic named-pair adjacent-cell selection criterion, remove ass:prespecified-score from the computational theorem and lem:negative-gain-cell-witness dependencies while retaining it for lem:finite-joint-validity, and synchronize proofs and prose.
